source: Learning bash with Rocky - Loops: while, until, and the exit/break/continue/true/false controls

questions.q: In any loop, the commands to repeat are placed between which words?
questions.why: Loop bodies sit between do and done.

questions.q: A while loop runs its body while the evaluated command is...
questions.why: while runs the body as long as the command returns true.

questions.q: An until loop runs its body while the command is...
options: false ($? != 0); it stops when the command becomes true
questions.why: until is the inverse of while.

questions.q: Which command ends the whole script immediately (optionally with a return code)?
questions.why: exit ends the script; the optional argument (0-255) sets the return code.

questions.q: In a loop, what do break and continue do?
options: break leaves the loop; continue jumps to the next iteration
questions.why: break goes to the first command after done; continue re-evaluates the loop.

questions.q: What does `while true; do ...; done` create?
options: An infinite loop (until a break or exit)
questions.why: true always returns 0, so the loop repeats forever; false always returns non-zero.
